item.title: HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this? — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this?

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-04-13 17:35 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

62107190
It's funny because I'm supposed to be an Europoor but my life standars is better than 99% of Americans lmfao.

I've never heard a single European complaining about debt. But you meet one of those Amerimutts that come to our continent for tourism or to teach English, and 5 minutes into the conversation they will start telling how how much in debt they are. BAFFLING.

>>62107169
>HCOL city
>$820 rent utilities included
and then your dad stopped molesting you and the out of body disassociation cope ended.

>>62107190
I'm American. Envious, ungrateful, whiny, bitchy Americans are an embarrassment. It's really not hard to live here at all. The problem is almost every retard that lives here thinks you should be able to afford a 2,300 sqft house on 1 acre of land, while having a new Toyota Camry in the driveway and enough in savings to go on 2 - 3 week-long vacations a year after putting 15% into an investment account, all on a McDonald's wage, or a basic-bitch corporate cubicle job, while only working 30 hours a week (yes, even 40 hours is too much work for Americans now).

Most people live WAY above their means. It's just a mixture of low-IQ combined with a set of personality traits that make them susceptible to short-term thinking and hedonism. Then they beg for free-gibs from the government while producing less material goods and services. It doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out where this leads to.

I feel zero pity or sympathy for any of these people. Fuck them and their retarded choices.

item.title: would /biz/bros invest in this — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
would /biz/bros invest in this

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-13 17:31 UTC

OP

- get 200,000$ seed capital
- buy RAM, SSD, GPU, some CPU (-5% from whole seller)
- open eshop, light marketing
- sell at 7% profit if someone happens to buy

- wait for price +50% eps. the DDR and SSD will most likely do that by 2027Q1
- sell -10% cheaper than competition (still 40% profit)
- after paying loans and operational costs, still 30% profit, ez+ 50,000$ (after startup costs)

top is in

>-5% wholesale?
congrats you discovered margins smaller than payment fees
>+7% sell?
PayPal + shipping + returns already ate that
>-10% vs competitors?
so you’re undercutting AND profiting? pick one retard


exit liquidity for amazon sellers top kek

>wait for +50% by 2027
ah yes the classic strategy of bagholding RAM sticks and praying for a cycle why not just buy micron stock and stop pretending you’re an entrepreneur

>30% profit after costs
you forgot:
• damaged stock
• dead inventory
• price drops mid cycle
• customers returning shit after opening it

real margin is 3–5% if you don’t fuck up and one bad batch is a full wipe

so you have no edge, no supplier control, no demand control, no moat
so know i wouldn't fucking invest in this

>NOT Polkadot's core network

>>62110141
The Mumbai warriors strike yet again. WE SHALL EAT WITH METAL SPOONS FOR MILLENNIA

62111510
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another point is that if this happened on the side of DOT then governance could just revert the transaction. Hackers can only get away with this kind of stuff on ETH because ETH doesn't have a good governance. The funds of EVERY exploit on DOT were recovered. We can't say that for ETH or for any other ecosystem.

It's laughable how people try to portray DOT as getting hacked when in reality the exploit happened on ETH, not DOT.

Look at the picture this guy posted >>62110117. Why are the looking at the exploit on etherscan, if it's a DOT exploit? How come we see "Transfer 108 ETH from Uniswap V4: Pool..."? Do you notice what I mean? The exploit is on ETH, not DOT. And even if the exploit happened on DOT governance would freeze the funds and revert the transactions.

62111757
>>62111458
But how did he gets away minting that much? Who was asleep at the wheel?

62111917
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another thing. The exploit happened on Hyperbridge, on a smart contract, on the side of Ethereum. And Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT. Do you notice how we're basically 2 hops away from DOT?. Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT (1st hop). Hyperbridge has smart contracts deployed on ETH in order for them to bridge between Hyperbridge and ETH (2nd hop). The exploit was an ETH smart contract exploit on Ethereum. If the same exploit happened on the side of Hyperbridge then the funds could EASILY be recovered through either the Hyperbridge governance (which is a copy of DOTs gov system. That's the whole point. That the corechains inherit DOTs functionality, therefore it's superior governance system) or the Polkadot governance.

>>62111757
Well.. Read the article https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/13/attacker-mints-usd1-billion-polkadot-tokens-on-ethereum-ends-up-stealing-just-usd250-000
>The accepted message executed changeAdmin on the bridged Polkadot token contract, transferring admin rights to the attacker's address. With admin control, the attacker minted 1 billion tokens in a single transaction and routed them through Odos Router V3 into a Uniswap V4 DOT-ETH pool, extracting roughly 108.2 ETH across what appears to be multiple swaps at slightly different prices.
He got the admin right to the contract, therefore he could do an infinite mint

Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

Nuclear power makes sense when there's high sustained demand.
Germany shares a border with 9 other countries. There's always the possibility of buying / selling electricity to balance the grid. Fluctuating renewables also make nuclear less attractive.
Then there's specialization going on inside the EU and France happens to make the nukes and the nuclear stuff. This way Germany can host the largest conventional military without becoming too threatening to its neighbors.
